Stucco Repair in Lee County, TX

Lee County's mix of sandy and clay soils, rural terrain, and warm, humid climate presents a distinct set of challenges for stucco surfaces. Star Stucco of Austin provides professional repair services across Giddings, Lexington, and the surrounding communities, addressing cracks, water damage, and failing finishes with techniques built for this part of Central Texas.

Get a Free EstimateCall (512) 706-9699

Stucco Conditions in Lee County

Lee County sits in the transition zone between the Blackland Prairie to the west and the Post Oak Belt to the east. The soils here are a mix of clay and sandy loam, which creates moderate but unpredictable foundation movement depending on exactly where your home sits. Properties on heavier clay experience more seasonal shifting, while those on sandier ground deal with different drainage patterns that can pool water against walls.

The warm, humid summers common in Lee County promote moisture-related stucco problems, including efflorescence, algae growth, and accelerated sealant failure. Properties surrounded by trees or in low-lying areas are particularly vulnerable because shade keeps walls damp longer after rain.

Lee County is a more rural area, and many properties sit on larger lots where landscaping, grading, and drainage are less controlled than in suburban developments. This can lead to water management issues that affect stucco performance over time. Our inspections consider the full picture — not just the stucco surface, but the grading, gutters, and drainage around the home.

What's Included With Our Stucco Repair in Lee County

Every project includes expert craftsmanship, quality materials, and a commitment to lasting results.

Property-Wide Inspection

We examine all stucco surfaces and assess the surrounding grading and drainage that may be contributing to moisture problems.

Moisture Testing

We test behind the stucco at damaged areas to determine if water has penetrated the wall and reached the substrate.

Damaged Stucco Removal

All failed material is removed to expose clean, sound substrate for the repair.

Substrate Repair

We repair or replace lath, building paper, and sheathing as needed to restore a solid base for the new stucco.

Color & Texture Matching

We match the repair to your existing stucco's color and surface texture so the fix blends in naturally.

Protective Finish & Seal

The repaired area is sealed to resist the humidity and moisture common in the Lee County climate.

Common Stucco Repair Needs in Lee County

These are the stucco repair issues we encounter most frequently on Lee County properties.

Cracking at Windows & Doors

Mixed soil conditions in Lee County create uneven settling that stresses stucco at openings. We rebuild these areas with reinforced mesh and flexible repair materials.

Water Damage & Mold

Humidity and poor drainage can trap moisture behind stucco, leading to mold growth and substrate rot. We open the wall, treat the mold, dry the substrate, and rebuild.

Patch Failures

Previous repairs that used incompatible materials or skipped prep work fail quickly in Lee County's humid climate. We strip the old patch and start fresh.

Delamination

Moisture cycling weakens the stucco-to-lath bond. We remove separated areas and rebuild with improved bonding.

Algae & Staining

Green algae and dark staining from tree shade and humidity are common. We clean the stucco, seal the surface, and address any moisture sources feeding the growth.

Weather & Impact Damage

Storms, fallen branches, and equipment damage leave chips and cracks. We restore the affected areas to match the surrounding finish.

Why Choose Us for Stucco Repair in Lee County?

  • We find the root cause of every repair need — soil movement, moisture, drainage, or material failure — and address it directly.
  • Our materials and methods are selected for Lee County's humid climate and variable soil conditions.
  • Color and texture matching is included in every repair for a consistent appearance.
  • Substrate and moisture problems are fixed before any new stucco is applied.
  • All work includes a written warranty covering materials and workmanship.
  • Free inspections throughout Lee County — call (512) 706-9699.

Licensed & Insured

Full liability coverage and workers' compensation for every project in Lee County.

20+ Years Experience

Decades of hands-on stucco work across Central Texas residential and commercial properties.

Certified Applicators

Certified by Sto Corp, Senergy/BASF, Master Wall, Dryvit, and Parex LaHabra for the highest quality results.

Our Stucco Repair Process

A straightforward approach from start to finish — no surprises, no shortcuts.

1

Schedule an Inspection

Call or submit a request online. We serve Giddings, Lexington, and the surrounding Lee County area.

2

Identify the Root Cause

Our technician inspects the stucco, tests for moisture, and evaluates drainage and soil conditions to pinpoint what is causing the damage.

3

Review the Estimate

We provide a written repair plan and cost breakdown. You know exactly what we are going to do and what it costs before we start.

4

Get the Repair Done

We remove the damaged stucco, restore the substrate, and apply a sealed, color-matched finish. We walk you through the completed work.
